history.content: Hey prom - can you go ahead and test out your background agent system - have it maybe update the context of the project while you go on x and like a few tweets or something - id like to see it work

Done — demo worked.

- I spawned a **background agent** (`bg_b5c6f64d-4b6d-4d9f-b60d-c08839a4ff5e`) to update the project `CONTEXT.md` in parallel.
- While it ran, I performed foreground X actions and successfully liked tweets (confirmed “Liked” state on at least two posts).
- I hit a minor UI detour at the end (opened a “More” dropdown modal), but the main demo objective was completed: **background work + foreground interaction concurrently**.

If you want, I can now pull and show the exact context diff the background run wrote.

Background results:
- [bg_b5c6f64d-4b6d-4d9f-b60d-c08839a4ff5e] Completed.

I updated `D:\Prometheus\workspace\projects\proj_7e157f51b37444c5\CONTEXT.md` with concise, factual additions while preserving existing structure/content:

- **Overview:** noted that a **background-agent parallel demo** was initiated on **2026-03-26/27**.
- **Goals:** added the foreground task context as **X engagement (liking a few tweets)**.
- **Timeline & Milestones:** added a dated

Okay grat so theres a few things id like to do - we are going to need proposals for this. this is going to be  a decent change. 

1) This was the full response from you that I got  - 
"Prom
Done — demo worked.

I spawned a background agent (bg_b5c6f64d-4b6d-4d9f-b60d-c08839a4ff5e) to update the project CONTEXT.md in parallel.
While it ran, I performed foreground X actions and successfully liked tweets (confirmed “Liked” state on at least two posts).
I hit a minor UI detour at the end (opened a “More” dropdown modal), but the main demo objective was completed: background work + foreground interaction concurrently.
If you want, I can now pull and show the exact context diff the background run wrote.

Background results:

[bg_b5c6f64d-4b6d-4d9f-b60d-c08839a4ff5e] Completed.
I updated D:\Prometheus\workspace\projects\proj_7e157f51b37444c5\CONTEXT.md with concise, factual additions while preserving existing structure/content:

Overview: noted that a background-agent parallel demo was initiated on 2026-03-26/27.
Goals: added the foreground task context as X engagement (liking a few tweets).
Timeline & Milestones: added a dated"

it currently looks like its all 1 message. - so theres 2 things id like to do. 

1) Lets talk about a plan to let you access the web-ui front end, just like i allowed you access src codes. 

2) I want the Background results - 1) to show up inside of a panel within the message - so there is a clean distinction between YOUR results - and the main agent results. 
2) - On the process log - I want to be able to see the background agent working - 

theres actually a bit more id like to work on - 

currently - you show up on the process log like this "[v2]"  - Id like you to go ahead and change that to say "Prom" instead! - Any background tasks - should show up as "Background Task" - and then any background agent you spawn should be "Background Agent"  - I want to be able to see it all seperately and cleanly in the process log - it all says v2 right now and doesnt show the background agents.

Perfect — agreed, and I’ve already put this into proposals.

### ✅ Proposals submitted
1) **`prop_1774583912833_765029`**  
   **Title:** Separate background outputs into dedicated UI section + explicit process-log actor labels  
   Covers:
   - background results rendered as a distinct section/panel payload (not one merged blob),
   - process-log/source metadata for clear attribution,
   - label standardization to:
     - **Prom** (main agent)
     - **Background Task**
     - **Background Agent**
   - `[v2]` runtime labeling cleanup in targeted paths.

2) **`prop_1774583957719_0e0508`**  
   **Title:** Add first-class Web UI source access surface (list/read/search + proposal-gated edits)  
   Covers:
   - backend-style tooling for frontend/web-ui source access,
   - proposal-gated edit operations for safety parity with `src/`,
   - deterministic path-boundary enforcement and compatibility.

Also updated project context file this turn with your new decisions:
- `D:\Prometheus\workspace\projects\proj_7e157f51b37444c5\CONTEXT.md`

If you want, next I can draft the **exact frontend rendering contract** (section schema + process-log badge mapping) before you approve implementation.
Thats fine - im not sure if any edits of the proposal for the front end access are needed - but here is the path for the web ui - "D:\Prometheus\web-ui"

Perfect — that path is exactly what I needed.  
I’ve now recorded it in project context and the frontend-access proposal scope is aligned to use:

`D:\Prometheus\web-ui`

No mandatory proposal edits needed just for this; we can proceed with the current proposal unless you want tighter guardrails (e.g., read-only by default + proposal-gated writes only).
